name: Restore Linear PTX Export Order, Then Peel The Fixed-K Steady State

summary.name: Restore Linear PTX Export Order, Then Peel The Fixed-K Steady State
summary.hypothesis: Round 65 should be treated as a closed negative on export traversal order, not as a new base: restoring grouped_rows=8 while reversing the PTX hot-band export traversal regressed to 25.69215965 ms even though dram, barrier, and long_scoreboard improved slightly versus round 64. That points to the export-order reversal itself being harmful, while grouped_rows=8 remains the accepted locality base. The next best move is therefore to restore the accepted active base exactly: grouped_rows=8, PTX hot-band right-left 64x64 consume order, reversed PTX compute row-pair traversal, original linear export traversal, one-sync steady-state handoff, B-first refill, and unroll 2. Once that accepted base is back, the highest-upside still-open family is fixed-shape steady-state peeling for the PTX hot-band K loop: split the fixed 452 K-tiles into prologue, steady-state, and epilogue so the active loop stops paying generic next_tile and future_tile control every iteration while keeping the current K16 double buffer unchanged.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Hot-loop orchestration in the active PTX 128x128 K16 kernel, especially barrier plus long_scoreboard overhead from the generic fixed-K loop handoff rather than raw DRAM bandwidth or a new tiling problem. The metrics still show low dram throughput and acceptable tensor activity, so the most plausible remaining headroom is fixed-shape control-path cleanup inside the existing two-stage pipeline.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:1964 stage_b_shared_tile_async B-first prologue in the active PTX kernel
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:1980 active PTX hot-band K loop with one-sync steady-state handoff
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:2005 B-first refill path inside the accepted PTX steady state
summary.risk: Medium. This direction touches the fixed-shape pipeline schedule in the active kernel, so it can easily reintroduce the already-closed K32 cadence or extra-live staging mistakes if the peeling is not kept strictly within the accepted K16 double-buffer structure. It must not reopen A-first refill, unroll-1, or any consumer-order sweep variants.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if restoring the accepted export order and peeling the fixed-K loop does not beat the round-58 accepted best of 24.57088089 ms, or if barrier plus long_scoreboard fail to improve without a compensating tensor-activity gain.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
